stem: A IT operations data backup uses 4 parallel storage devices for redundancy. The data recoverable requires at least one storage device to function. storage device 1 has a reliability between 74% and 91% (uncertain due to limited operational data). storage device 2 has a reliability of 91%. storage device 3 has a reliability of 96%. storage device 4 has a reliability of 84%. Additionally, there is a common-cause failure probability (e.g., power outage, shared software bug) that could disable all storage devices simultaneously, estimated between 4.8% and 7.9%. What is the overall probability that the data recoverable?

ground_truth.answer: P(data recoverable) ranges from 0.9209 to 0.9519. Point estimate: 0.9359.

solution_steps: P(system works) = 1 - P(all 4 storage devices fail simultaneously).
With common-cause failure (4.8%-7.9%): P(works) = (1-P_cc) × P(independent works). Range: [0.9209, 0.9519].
common_failure_modes: Multiplying individual reliabilities instead of computing 1 - P(all fail)
Using a single reliability for the uncertain component without propagating uncertainty
Treating the system as a series (all must work) rather than parallel (any one suffices)
Ignoring common-cause failures that can disable all paths simultaneously
